title: Asking for Directions
summary: A visitor asks a pedestrian for directions to the nearest post office and the pedestrian gives clear, step-by-step guidance.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: Excuse me, could you help me? I'm looking for the post office.

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: Of course! The post office isn't far from here.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: That's a relief. I've been walking for twenty minutes and I'm completely lost.

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: Don't worry. Walk straight along this road until you reach the traffic lights.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: Straight ahead to the traffic lights — got it. And then?

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: Turn left at the traffic lights. You'll pass a small supermarket on your right.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: Turn left, supermarket on the right. Okay, I think I can remember that.

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: After the supermarket, keep walking for about two minutes. The post office is next to a big red church.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: A red church — that sounds easy to spot. How long will it take me to walk there?

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: It should take about ten minutes on foot from here.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: Perfect. Oh, one more thing — do you know what time the post office closes today?

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: I believe it closes at five o'clock. You should have plenty of time.

body.speaker: Visitor
body.text: Wonderful. Thank you so much for your help.

body.speaker: Pedestrian
body.text: You're very welcome. Enjoy your visit to our town!

annotations.meaningVi: Thật may mắn / Thật là nhẹ nhõm
annotations.exampleEn: I found my passport — that's a relief!
We were completely lost in the new city.
Walk straight along the main road for five minutes.
Turn right when you reach the traffic lights.
Turn left at the corner and the hotel is on your right.
The library is on your right after the bridge.
The yellow building is easy to spot from the road.
It takes fifteen minutes on foot from the station.

annotations.meaningVi: nhiều thời gian, đủ thời gian
annotations.exampleEn: Don't rush — we have plenty of time before the train.

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi tin rằng / Tôi nghĩ là
annotations.exampleEn: I believe the shop opens at nine.

annotations.meaningVi: Chúc bạn có chuyến thăm vui vẻ

grammarNotes.title: Imperative for giving directions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi chỉ đường, chúng ta thường dùng dạng mệnh lệnh cách: động từ nguyên thể không có chủ ngữ. Ví dụ: 'Walk straight along this road.', 'Turn left at the traffic lights.', 'Keep walking for two minutes.' Đây là cách tự nhiên và ngắn gọn nhất để hướng dẫn ai đó.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the imperative form (base verb, no subject) to give directions. Examples: 'Walk straight along this road.', 'Turn left at the lights.', 'Keep walking for two minutes.' This is the most natural and concise way to guide someone.

grammarNotes.title: Prepositions of place: next to, on your right, past
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để mô tả vị trí khi chỉ đường, ta dùng các giới từ chỉ nơi chốn. 'Next to' = bên cạnh ('The post office is next to a red church.'), 'on your right/left' = bên phải/trái bạn ('There is a supermarket on your right.'), 'past' = đi qua ('Walk past the supermarket.').
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use prepositions of place to describe locations. 'Next to' = beside ('The post office is next to a church.'), 'on your right/left' = on the right/left side as you walk ('The supermarket is on your right.'), 'past' = further than ('Walk past the supermarket.').

translationVi:
Khách: Xin lỗi, bạn có thể giúp tôi không? Tôi đang tìm bưu điện.
Người đi đường: Tất nhiên rồi! Bưu điện không xa đây lắm đâu.
Khách: Thật may mắn. Tôi đã đi bộ hai mươi phút rồi và bị lạc hoàn toàn.
Người đi đường: Đừng lo lắng. Đi thẳng theo con đường này cho đến khi bạn đến đèn giao thông.
Khách: Đi thẳng đến đèn giao thông — hiểu rồi. Rồi sao?
Người đi đường: Rẽ trái tại đèn giao thông. Bạn sẽ đi qua một siêu thị nhỏ ở bên phải.
Khách: Rẽ trái, siêu thị ở bên phải. Được rồi, tôi nghĩ tôi có thể nhớ điều đó.
Người đi đường: Sau siêu thị, tiếp tục đi khoảng hai phút. Bưu điện ở cạnh một nhà thờ màu đỏ lớn.
Khách: Nhà thờ màu đỏ — nghe có vẻ dễ nhận ra. Đi bộ đến đó mất bao lâu?
Người đi đường: Đi bộ từ đây khoảng mười phút.
Khách: Tuyệt. Ồ, còn một điều nữa — bạn có biết hôm nay bưu điện đóng cửa lúc mấy giờ không?
Người đi đường: Tôi nghĩ nó đóng lúc năm giờ. Bạn sẽ có đủ thời gian.
Khách: Tuyệt vời. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều vì đã giúp đỡ.
Người đi đường: Không có gì. Chúc bạn có chuyến thăm thị trấn của chúng tôi vui vẻ!

questions.prompt: What is the visitor looking for?
questions.explanation: The visitor says 'I'm looking for the post office.'
questions.hint: The visitor states what they need at the very beginning.

questions.prompt: How long has the visitor been walking before asking for help?
questions.explanation: The visitor says 'I've been walking for twenty minutes.'
questions.hint: The visitor mentions a specific number of minutes.

questions.prompt: What is the first direction the pedestrian gives?
options: Walk straight along the road to the traffic lights
Cross the road and turn right
Go back the way you came
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'Walk straight along this road until you reach the traffic lights.'
questions.hint: The pedestrian tells the visitor what to do first.

questions.prompt: Which direction should the visitor turn at the traffic lights?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'Turn left at the traffic lights.'
questions.hint: Left or right at the lights?

questions.prompt: What will the visitor pass after turning at the lights?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'You'll pass a small supermarket on your right.'
questions.hint: It is a shop that appears on the right side.

questions.prompt: Where is the supermarket positioned relative to the visitor?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'a small supermarket on your right.'
questions.hint: Listen for which side the pedestrian mentions.

questions.prompt: What is next to the post office?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'The post office is next to a big red church.'
questions.hint: A landmark is mentioned to help identify the post office.

questions.prompt: How long does it take to walk to the post office from where they are standing?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'It should take about ten minutes on foot from here.'
questions.hint: The pedestrian gives an estimated walking time.

questions.prompt: What does the visitor want to know at the end of the conversation?
options: What time the post office closes
Where the nearest bus stop is
questions.explanation: The visitor asks 'do you know what time the post office closes today?'
questions.hint: The visitor asks one more question about opening hours.

questions.prompt: What time does the pedestrian think the post office closes?
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'I believe it closes at five o'clock.'
questions.hint: The pedestrian is not completely certain but gives a time.

questions.prompt: How does the pedestrian end the conversation?
options: By offering to walk with the visitor
By wishing the visitor a good trip home
By wishing the visitor an enjoyable visit to the town
By asking where the visitor is from
questions.explanation: The pedestrian says 'Enjoy your visit to our town!'
questions.hint: The final line is a warm farewell related to the visitor's stay.

cloze.template:
Visitor: Excuse me, could you help me? I'm looking for the {{b1}} office.
Pedestrian: Of course! The post office isn't far from here.
Visitor: That's a relief. I've been walking for twenty minutes and I'm completely {{b2}}.
Pedestrian: Don't worry. Walk {{b3}} along this road until you reach the traffic lights.
Visitor: Straight ahead to the traffic lights — got it. And then?
Pedestrian: Turn {{b4}} at the traffic lights. You'll pass a small supermarket on your right.
Visitor: Turn left, supermarket on the right. Okay, I think I can remember that.
Pedestrian: After the supermarket, keep walking for about two minutes. The post office is {{b5}} to a big red church.
Visitor: A red church — that sounds easy to {{b6}}. How long will it take me to walk there?
Pedestrian: It should take about ten minutes on {{b7}} from here.
Visitor: Perfect. Oh, one more thing — do you know what time the post office {{b8}} today?
Pedestrian: I believe it closes at five o'clock. You should have {{b9}} of time.
Visitor: Wonderful. Thank you so much for your {{b10}}.
Pedestrian: You're very welcome. Enjoy your visit to our town!

blanks.explanation: 'post' — the visitor is looking for the post office.
'lost' — the visitor says they are completely lost after walking for twenty minutes.
'straight' — the direction is to walk straight along the road.
'left' — the instruction is to turn left at the traffic lights.
'next' — the post office is next to a big red church.
'spot' — the visitor says the red church sounds easy to spot (easily noticed).
'foot' — on foot means walking; it takes ten minutes on foot.
'closes' — the visitor asks what time the post office closes.
'plenty' — the pedestrian says the visitor will have plenty of time.
'help' — the visitor thanks the pedestrian for their help.

criticalThinkingQuestion: With smartphones and map apps widely available, fewer people ask strangers for directions. Do you think this has made us less connected to the communities we live in and visit? What do we gain or lose when we rely on technology instead of talking to people?
